spark:position-deletes:v2.notes: Full support for position delete files as the reference Iceberg implementation

spark:position-deletes:v3.notes: Full support including V3 binary deletion vectors for more efficient row-level deletes
caveats: Deletion vectors (V3) require Iceberg 1.6+ and Spark 3.5+ or 4.0+

spark:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Full support for equality delete files as the reference Iceberg implementation

spark:equality-deletes:v3.notes: Full equality delete support with V3 tables

spark:merge-on-read:v2.notes: Full merge-on-read support with position and equality deletes; configurable via write.delete.mode and write.update.mode table properties

spark:merge-on-read:v3.notes: Full merge-on-read support with V3 deletion vectors for improved performance
caveats: Deletion vectors require Iceberg 1.6+ and Spark 3.5+ or 4.0+

spark:copy-on-write:v2.notes: Full copy-on-write support; default mode for DELETE, UPDATE, and MERGE operations

spark:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Full copy-on-write support with V3 tables

spark:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Full schema evolution via ALTER TABLE: add, drop, rename, reorder columns, widen types (int→long, float→double), and modify nullability

spark:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Full schema evolution support with V3 tables, including automatic schema merging on write

spark:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values (initial-default, write-default) are a V3 feature

spark:column-default-values:v3.notes: Iceberg V3 spec supports initial-default and write-default at the metadata level. Spark's Iceberg integration can read/write tables with column defaults, but Spark SQL DDL does not yet expose full syntax for managing column defaults on Iceberg tables
caveats: No Spark SQL DDL syntax for setting Iceberg column defaults
Requires Iceberg 1.6+ with format-version 3

spark:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature

spark:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Spark does not support Iceberg nanosecond timestamp types (timestamp_ns, timestamptz_ns). The Iceberg-to-Spark type conversion table lists these as 'Not supported'. Creating or reading V3 tables with nanosecond timestamp columns will fail
caveats: Spark has no native nanosecond timestamp type
See GitHub issue apache/iceberg#13681

spark:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Full hidden partitioning support with transforms: year, month, day, hour, bucket, truncate. Users query by data values without knowing partition layout

spark:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Full hidden partitioning support with V3 tables

spark:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Full partition evolution via ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP/REPLACE PARTITION FIELD. Metadata-only operation; existing data is not rewritten
caveats: Requires Iceberg SQL extensions

spark:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Full partition evolution support with V3 tables
caveats: Requires Iceberg SQL extensions

spark:multi-arg-transforms:v2.notes: Multi-argument (multi-source-column) transforms are a V3 feature

spark:multi-arg-transforms:v3.notes: V3 introduces multi-argument transforms that can reference multiple source columns. Spark support is being developed; single-column transforms (bucket, truncate, year, month, day, hour) are fully supported
caveats: Multi-source-column transforms are a new V3 capability with evolving Spark support
Requires Spark 4.0+ for full V3 transform support

spark:time-travel:v2.notes: Full time travel support via SQL (TIMESTAMP AS OF, VERSION AS OF) and DataFrame API (snapshot-id, as-of-timestamp, branch, tag options). Supports branches and tags for versioned reads

spark:time-travel:v3.notes: Full time travel support with V3 tables, including branch and tag references

spark:table-maintenance:v2.notes: Full table maintenance via stored procedures: expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files, rewrite_data_files (with bin-pack and sort strategies including z-order), rewrite_manifests, and rewrite_position_delete_files
caveats: Requires Iceberg SQL extensions for stored procedures

spark:table-maintenance:v3.notes: Full table maintenance support with V3 tables
caveats: Requires Iceberg SQL extensions for stored procedures

spark:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Full branching and tagging support via DDL (CREATE/REPLACE/DROP BRANCH and TAG) and procedures (fast_forward, cherrypick_snapshot). Supports write-audit-publish (WAP) workflows and reading/writing to specific branches
caveats: Requires Iceberg SQL extensions

spark:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Full branching and tagging support with V3 tables
caveats: Requires Iceberg SQL extensions

spark:read-support:v2.notes: Full read support via SQL SELECT, DataFrame API, and metadata table inspection (history, snapshots, files, manifests, partitions, entries). Supports incremental reads and vectorized Parquet reading

spark:read-support:v3.notes: Full read support for V3 tables including deletion vectors. Spark is the most feature-rich engine for Iceberg V3 reads
caveats: Some V3 data types (nanosecond timestamps, geospatial) are not readable in Spark

spark:write-insert:v2.notes: Full write support: INSERT INTO, INSERT OVERWRITE (static and dynamic modes), DataFrameWriterV2 API (append, overwritePartitions), and CTAS/RTAS

spark:write-insert:v3.notes: Full write support for V3 tables including all insert modes

spark:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: Full row-level DML: MERGE INTO (with WHEN MATCHED, WHEN NOT MATCHED, WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE), UPDATE, DELETE FROM. Supports both copy-on-write and merge-on-read modes
caveats: MERGE INTO, UPDATE, and row-level DELETE require Iceberg SQL extensions

spark:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: Full DML support for V3 tables. Spark 4.0 adds DataFrame MERGE INTO API (mergeInto)
caveats: MERGE INTO, UPDATE, and row-level DELETE require Iceberg SQL extensions
DataFrame MERGE INTO API requires Spark 4.0+

spark:catalog-integration:v2.notes: Supports all major Iceberg catalog implementations via SparkCatalog and SparkSessionCatalog. Catalog types: hive, hadoop, rest, glue, jdbc, nessie, and custom implementations via catalog-impl

spark:catalog-integration:v3.notes: All catalog implementations support V3 tables

spark:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Hive Metastore catalog fully supports V3 tables

spark:aws-glue-catalog:v2.notes: AWS Glue Catalog supported natively via catalog type 'glue' with the iceberg-aws module

spark:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: AWS Glue Catalog supports V3 tables

spark:rest-catalog:v2.notes: Full REST Catalog support via catalog type 'rest'. Connects to any Iceberg REST Catalog-compatible endpoint

spark:nessie:v2.notes: Nessie catalog supported natively via catalog type 'nessie'. Provides Git-like version control for data tables

spark:polaris:v3.notes: Polaris supports V3 tables via REST Catalog API

spark:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Unity Catalog supported via REST Catalog API, as Unity Catalog implements the Iceberg REST Catalog specification

spark:unity-catalog:v3.notes: Unity Catalog supports V3 tables via REST Catalog API

spark:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: Native Hadoop catalog support via catalog type 'hadoop'. Uses a directory-based layout in HDFS or compatible file systems

spark:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: JDBC catalog supported natively via catalog type 'jdbc'. Stores Iceberg metadata in a relational database

flink:position-deletes:v2.notes: Flink supports reading and writing position deletes in both batch and streaming modes

flink:position-deletes:v3.notes: Position deletes supported via Iceberg library V3 support (iceberg-flink-runtime 1.10.x+)

flink:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Flink can read tables with evolved schemas, but ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties — column and partition changes are not supported via Flink DDL

flink:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Flink can read V3 tables with evolved schemas, but ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties — column and partition changes are not supported via Flink DDL
caveats: ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties; column additions, renames, drops, and reordering not supported via Flink SQL

flink:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature

flink:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Flink supports nanosecond timestamp precision, matching the full range of Iceberg's timestamp spec (Iceberg 1.11.0, Flink 2.1).

flink:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Flink can read and write to tables with hidden partitions created by other engines, but Flink DDL does not support creating tables with transform-based (hidden) partitioning

flink:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Partition evolution supported via the Dynamic Sink (Sink V2-based implementation) for V3 tables

flink:time-travel:v2.notes: Flink supports time travel via snapshot-id and as-of-timestamp read options in batch mode, plus branch and tag reads via SQL hints

databend:position-deletes:v3.notes: V3 positional delete handling is not documented in the Databend Iceberg engine docs

databend:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Read support for V2 equality delete files is not documented on the Databend Iceberg engine page; the docs do not describe whether or how Databend applies equality deletes at scan time

databend:merge-on-read:v2.notes: V2 merge-on-read read semantics (applying positional and equality delete files at scan time) are not documented for Databend's Iceberg engine; the engine page does not describe how Databend reconciles delete files when reading V2 tables

databend:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Databend's Iceberg engine documents only INSERT INTO and does not document V3 writes or any UPDATE/DELETE that would use copy-on-write to rewrite data files

databend:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Databend's Iceberg engine does not document ALTER TABLE schema-evolution operations (ADD/DROP/RENAME/REORDER COLUMN, type widening) on Iceberg tables; the documented ENGINE = ICEBERG surface covers only CREATE TABLE, INSERT INTO, and SELECT

databend:type-promotion:v2.notes: Type-promotion semantics on Iceberg tables (int→long, float→double, decimal precision widening) are not documented for Databend's Iceberg engine; the documented ENGINE = ICEBERG surface does not include ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN TYPE operations and the column-type mapping table only describes initial column types at CREATE TABLE time

databend:type-promotion:v3.notes: Type-promotion semantics on Iceberg tables are not documented for Databend, and Iceberg V3 format support is not documented on the Databend Iceberg engine page

databend:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values (initial-default, write-default) are an Iceberg V3 feature and are not defined in the V2 spec, so they do not apply to Databend on V2

databend:column-default-values:v3.notes: Iceberg V3 column-default-value semantics (initial-default and write-default field properties) are not documented for Databend; the Iceberg engine docs do not describe how the ENGINE = ICEBERG CREATE TABLE syntax interacts with V3 column defaults, and V3 format support itself is not documented

databend:table-creation:v2.notes: Databend documents CREATE TABLE ... ENGINE = ICEBERG with an optional PARTITION BY clause for identity-only partition columns; the documented surface covers initial table creation against an attached Iceberg catalog but does not extend to advanced table options

databend:table-creation:v3.notes: Targeting Iceberg V3 format on CREATE TABLE is not documented for Databend; the ENGINE = ICEBERG CREATE TABLE syntax does not document a 'format-version' table property and V3 format support itself is not documented on the Iceberg engine page

databend:time-travel:v2.notes: The Databend Iceberg engine docs do not document time-travel syntax for Iceberg tables; the AT (SNAPSHOT => ...) clause is documented only for the Fuse engine and not for ENGINE = ICEBERG tables, and the only documented way to access Iceberg snapshot or manifest metadata is via the ICEBERG_SNAPSHOT and ICEBERG_MANIFEST table functions, which inspect metadata rather than read prior table states

databend:time-travel:v3.notes: Time-travel on Iceberg tables is not documented for Databend on V3; the AT (SNAPSHOT => ...) clause is documented only for the Fuse engine, the ICEBERG_SNAPSHOT and ICEBERG_MANIFEST table functions only expose metadata inspection, and Iceberg V3 format support itself is not documented on the Databend Iceberg engine page

databend:table-maintenance:v2.notes: The Databend Iceberg engine docs do not document maintenance actions on Iceberg tables: there is no documented compaction, snapshot expiry, manifest rewrite, or rollback procedure exposed through ENGINE = ICEBERG, and the ICEBERG_SNAPSHOT and ICEBERG_MANIFEST table functions only inspect metadata without rewriting it

databend:table-maintenance:v3.notes: The Databend Iceberg engine docs do not document maintenance actions on Iceberg V3 tables (compaction, snapshot expiry, manifest rewrite, rollback are all absent from the documented surface), and Iceberg V3 format support itself is not documented on the Databend Iceberg engine page

databend:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Branch and tag management on Iceberg tables is not documented for Databend; the experimental ALTER TABLE ...
